spec_match.rationale: The page provides a detailed explanation of DOCTYPE triggers, quirks mode effects, and lists exactly how CompatMode identifies BackCompat vs CSS1Compat.

interactive.rationale: The page is largely static, and the 'Run capability probe' button just dumps unrelated general web APIs (like WebGPU, customElements, etc.) rather than demonstrating quirks-mode anchor matching or DOCTYPE-specific behaviors.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The explanation is well-aligned with the rationale behind fixing the case-insensitive matching quirks mode bug for interoperability.

edge_cases.rationale: The concept page contains no functional edge-case test suite or live mode toggles, relying purely on static text and copy-paste probe logic.

references.rationale: Cites MDN and ChromeStatus links correctly.

design_system.rationale: Integrates correctly with the styling, table formatting, and color design guidelines.

openQuestions.title: Remove unrelated capability probe boilerplate
openQuestions.detail: The current live capability probe scans for WebGPU, mediaDevices, and credential APIs, which are completely unrelated to quirks mode fragment matching. This boilerplate should be removed or replaced with actual fragment matching capability checks.

openQuestions.title: Implement a live DOCTYPE/quirks-mode interactive tester
openQuestions.detail: Instead of static cards, the page should let users load an iframe either in standards mode (with doctype) or quirks mode (without doctype) and observe fragment matching behavior live to make it genuinely interactive.

summary: An informative explanation page that suffers from unrelated boilerplate copy-paste probe JS code and a lack of actual functional interactivity.
